conjecture_title: Open Question: NP-hardness of List-k-Coloring on rP3-free graphs
paper_title: Complexity dichotomy for List-5-Coloring with a forbidden induced subgraph
summary: The open question asks whether List-$k$-Coloring on $rP_3$-free graphs is NP-hard for some $k\in\mathbb{N}$. arXiv:2311.05713 (Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl; Combinatorica 2024) proves a full complexity dichotomy for all $k>4$: List-$k$-Coloring on $H$-free graphs is polynomial-time solvable whenever every component of $H$ is a path on at most three vertices, and $rP_3$ satisfies this condition. Combined with the source paper's polynomial result for $k=5$ and classical results for $k\leq 4$, List-$k$-Coloring on $rP_3$-free graphs is polynomial-time solvable for all $k\in\mathbb{N}$, answering the open question in the negative.

since_posted.title: List-k-Coloring H-free graphs for all k>4
since_posted.authors: Maria Chudnovsky, Sepehr Hajebi, Sophie Spirkl
since_posted.claim: Proves that for all k>4, List-k-Coloring on H-free graphs is polynomial-time solvable when every component of H is a path on at most 3 vertices (covering all rP3), thereby establishing that no k exists for which List-k-Coloring on rP3-free graphs is NP-hard.

since_posted.title: Maximum List r-Colorable Induced Subgraphs in kP3-Free Graphs
since_posted.authors: Esther Galby, Paloma T. Lima, Andrea Munaro, Amir Nikabadi
since_posted.claim: Generalizes polynomial-time solvability to Max-Weight List r-Colorable Induced Subgraph on kP3-free graphs for all fixed r and k, providing a short self-contained proof of List r-Coloring polynomial-time solvability as a special case.

internal_refs_verified.note: Confirmed via arXiv abstract page: Chudnovsky, Hajebi, Spirkl prove the full dichotomy for List-k-Coloring H-free graphs for k>4, with polynomial-time solvability for rP3-free graphs for all k>4. Published in Combinatorica 44 (2024).

search_queries: List-k-Coloring rP3-free graphs NP-hard complexity Hajebi Spirkl
arXiv 2105.01787 List-5-Coloring forbidden induced subgraph cite follow-up
Chudnovsky Hajebi "List-k-Coloring H-Free Graphs" "k>4" arXiv polynomial rP3
notes: The open question is definitively answered in the negative: List-k-Coloring on rP3-free graphs is polynomial-time solvable for all k in N. The key paper arXiv:2311.05713 was already present in the supplied internal references (2311.05713__01). The 2025 paper arXiv:2505.00412 further generalizes the result to the weighted setting.
